en: Behold, a king will reign in righteousness, and princes will rule in justice.
zh: 看哪！必有一王憑公義執政， 必有眾領袖按公平治理。

en: Each will be like a hiding place from the wind, a shelter from the storm, like streams of water in a dry place, like the shade of a great rock in a weary land.
zh: 必有一人像避風所， 和避暴雨的隱密處， 像乾旱之地的溪水， 又像疲乏之地的大磐石的陰影。

en: Then the eyes of those who see will not be closed, and the ears of those who hear will give attention.
zh: 那時，能看見事物者的眼睛，必不再昏暗； 能聽到事物者的耳朵，必然傾聽。

en: The heart of the hasty will understand and know, and the tongue of the stammerers will hasten to speak distinctly.
zh: 性急者的心必明白知識， 口吃者的舌頭必說話清楚。

en: The fool will no more be called noble, nor the scoundrel said to be honorable.
zh: 愚頑人不再被稱為高尚， 惡棍也不再被稱為大方。

en: For the fool speaks folly, and his heart is busy with iniquity, to practice ungodliness, to utter error concerning the LORD, to leave the craving of the hungry unsatisfied, and to deprive the thirsty of drink.
zh: 因為愚頑人說的是愚頑話， 他心裡所想的是罪孽， 慣行褻瀆 神的事，說錯謬的話攻擊耶和華， 使飢餓的人仍空著肚子， 使口渴的人仍無水可喝。

en: As for the scoundrel- his devices are evil; he plans wicked schemes to ruin the poor with lying words, even when the plea of the needy is right.
zh: 至於惡棍，他的手段是邪惡的， 他圖謀惡計， 用虛假的言語毀滅困苦的人， 即使在窮乏人講公理的時候，他也是這樣。

en: But he who is noble plans noble things, and on noble things he stands.
zh: 高尚的人卻籌謀高尚的事， 他也必堅持這些高尚的事。

en: Rise up, you women who are at ease, hear my voice; you complacent daughters, give ear to my speech.
zh: 安逸的婦女啊！你們要起來， 聽我的聲音； 無憂無慮的女子啊！ 你們要側耳聽我的話。

en: In little more than a year you will shudder, you complacent women; for the grape harvest fails, the fruit harvest will not come.
zh: 無憂無慮的女子啊！再過一年多， 你們就必受困擾， 因為沒有葡萄可摘， 收禾稼的日子也沒有來。

en: Tremble, you women who are at ease, shudder, you complacent ones; strip, and make yourselves bare, and tie sackcloth around your waist.
zh: 安逸的婦女啊！你們要受震驚。 無憂無慮的女子啊！你們要受困擾了， 脫去衣服，赤著身子，以麻布束腰吧。

en: Beat your breasts for the pleasant fields, for the fruitful vine,
zh: 你們要為美好的田地和多結果子的葡萄樹搥胸哀哭。

en: for the soil of my people growing up in thorns and briers, yes, for all the joyous houses in the exultant city.
zh: 也為那些在我子民的土地上長起來的荊棘和蒺藜而哀哭， 為那歡樂的城和所有快樂的房屋，也是這樣。

en: For the palace is forsaken, the populous city deserted; the hill and the watchtower will become dens forever, a joy of wild donkeys, a pasture of flocks;
zh: 因為宮殿必被丟棄，熱鬧的城市也被撇下， 山岡和守望樓必永遠成為洞穴， 作了野驢喜歡的地方和羊群的草場。

en: until the Spirit is poured upon us from on high, and the wilderness becomes a fruitful field, and the fruitful field is deemed a forest.
zh: 等到聖靈從高處傾倒在我們身上， 曠野變為肥田， 肥田被看為樹林的時候，

en: Then justice will dwell in the wilderness, and righteousness abide in the fruitful field.
zh: 公平就必居在曠野中， 公義必住在肥田裡。

en: And the effect of righteousness will be peace, and the result of righteousness, quietness and trust forever.
zh: 公義的果效必是平安， 公義的效驗必是平靜與安穩，直到永遠。

en: My people will abide in a peaceful habitation, in secure dwellings, and in quiet resting places.
zh: 那時，我的子民必住在平安的居所， 安穩的住處，不受騷擾的安息之處。

en: And it will hail when the forest falls down, and the city will be utterly laid low.
zh: 但在敵人的國土中，必有冰雹降下，打倒他們的樹林， 他們的城必被夷平。

en: Happy are you who sow beside all waters, who let the feet of the ox and the donkey range free.
zh: 你們這些在各水邊撒種， 又使牛驢隨意走動的，是多麼有福啊！
